So long as the US wants to be retarded and use assets as weapons commodity producers and buyers will trend towards things that the US cannot manipulate, the underlying citizens do they same for the most part. item.title: Attainable careers/skills for money — 6 replies — 6.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Attainable careers/skills for money

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-26 07:51 UTC

OP

Everywhere you look they see it’s not worth it to do or train for xyz job especially if it’s tech or admin related
But it’s hard to even tell what is true or not anymore some just want to gatekeep or fear monger
Originally I wanted to get into UI/UX design but I keep hearing it’s getting more and more reduced to a function than a full position thanks to AI
Please help is there something no BS I can learn with actual gainful employment at the end and possibly to work for myself or freelance?

Early replies (first 3)

62273250
>>62273242
med or law

everything else is shit really, high finance is good but you need nepo connections. /med/ is the ultimate equalizer, if you are competent and do good u will make a shitload of money in a high risk specialty

62273265
fuck off find your niche

62273376
I became an accountant because of a biz thread posted here in 2023

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273378
>>62273242
Pressure washer.

62273431
>>62273242
Plumbing or HVAC will always need people. Electricians never have to sweep up or deal with shit from the dirty plumbing jobs. Also worth looking into is going to oilfield or offshore working and investmaxing until you make it.

62274054
>>62273242
Do you have relatives or acquaintances willing to help you land a job in their field? Do that. If you have no one, go for trades or FIFO. Just go for something more specialized like being an electrician, or doing HVAC, plumbing, welding, heavy equipment, forklift, etc. Don't go for some shit any Mexican straight off the streets can do, like carpentry, roofing, or concrete. I am an electrician, and once you get over the first two years, it gets much easier and you get more options. I have a semi-office job for a median wage, and I haven't touched any serious work yet this month. All I am doing is drinking coffee and shitposting on 4chan. And as far as FIFO is concerned, I heard from my friend that electricians can get up to $200k on some shit-ass oil rig. But I prefer being healthy over money. I already have everything anyway. Please, just don't take student loans to get some shit degree. Simply not having a studen loan gives You such huge head start compared to Your peers.

I live in the most conservative part of a really red state but I've never seen so many "I regret voting for Trump" signs in lawns. Everywhere I look, not one Trump supporter who doesn't regret it. It's crazy because everybody here voted Trump 3 times in a row, but it's obvious that this time, it was a mistake. We call him "Trump the Mistake" in my house.

I was at a bar in town on Saturday night. It's the kind of place where working class guys, truckers, and farmers go to unwind after an honest day's work, a real salt-of-the-Earth type of place. We were watching a local high school football game on the TV and one of the players happened to be named Harris, which prompted people in the bar to start talking about Kamala Harris. My dad (a coal-miner) says "y'know, I've voted red since the Reagan administration but I really should have given her a chance. I'm a fool for being so racist and sexist that I couldn't vote for a Brown woman, she was clearly the best choice." Then the local gun store owner, who is also the head of the local KKK chapter, said “I can’t believe I’m saying this, but we need to impeach Trump and Vance and replace them with Kamala.” I look around and my face is stunned. All of these hardcore Trump supporters are shaking their heads in agreement. I heard a couple “yes, sir” and “damn straight”. Everyone started nodding and agreeing, and we were talking about how she's not a billionaire so she understands the plight of the middle class. We all call her Middle Class Kamala around these parts.

She was the best candidate for the job, and the only reason she lost is because we were too racist and sexist to realize it. She was basically President for the last 4 years and our economy was booming. We call her Queen Kamala and praise her Kamalaconomy. Anyways just thought I'd throw in my two cents on the matter

Everything will hum along until demand destruction cannot be ignored on bottom lines. The talking heads remain gleeful for now because spending is up so everything goes higher. Normally demand destruction and the following recession would be self corrective, prices would realign, asset and energy prices fall, businesses fall and new ones sprout up. Now though fuel prices cannot go down, good prices are fucked by this new floor, electricity prices will remain high they are already are trying to ration by price now. The barrier to entry for new businesses, places for the newly unemployed were previously given new jobs in 08, is semi-permanently raised and less opportunities arise, some roles now going to AI. The consumer was already buying inferior goods and cutting back, getting mortgage assistance, missing payments. Something like 1 in 3 has had utilities cut off for non-payment. All this while the economy is "humming". the reversal will be biblical and it's a well deserved straw breaking the camels back.

Bailouts aren't feasible like in 08, they couldn't save everything if they wanted to, there's 2-3x more AI companies than should logically exist but they were providing many jobs via capex, soon to be gone. Inflationary pressures will remain into a crash should it come and and it will claim more victims. Government assistance, if any is had, will only exacerbate things. These bullshit companies trying to sell services to a permafucked consumer will be funny to watch. I'm thinking about starting a security company.
